nodes.title: Wave Interference
nodes.description: Superpose two wave sources and watch constructive and destructive interference build a standing pattern.

nodes.title: Blackbody Radiation
nodes.description: Planck's quantized oscillators vs. the classical Rayleigh-Jeans catastrophe — the birth of quantum theory.

nodes.title: Photoelectric Effect
nodes.description: Einstein's photon: light ejects electrons only above a threshold frequency, regardless of intensity.

nodes.title: Double-Slit Experiment
nodes.description: Single particles build a wave interference pattern — the canonical demonstration of wave-particle duality.

nodes.title: Particle in a Box
nodes.description: Quantized energy levels emerge when a particle is confined — the simplest exactly-solvable quantum system.

nodes.title: Uncertainty Principle
nodes.description: Heisenberg's Δx·Δp ≥ ħ/2 — position and momentum cannot be simultaneously sharp.

nodes.title: Quantum Tunneling
nodes.description: A quantum wavefunction penetrates a classically forbidden barrier — the basis of nuclear fusion and tunnel diodes.

nodes.title: Bell's Inequality
nodes.description: Correlation experiments that rule out local hidden-variable theories — entanglement is real.

nodes.title: Quantum Eraser
nodes.description: Erasing which-path information restores interference — measurement, not disturbance, collapses the wavefunction.

nodes.title: Maxwell-Boltzmann Distribution
nodes.description: The equilibrium speed distribution of an ideal gas — the foundation of statistical mechanics.

nodes.title: Entropy & Microstates
nodes.description: Boltzmann's S = k ln W — entropy as a count of microscopic arrangements.

nodes.title: Lévy Flights & Brownian Motion
nodes.description: From Gaussian random walks to heavy-tailed Lévy flights — stochastic transport across scales.

nodes.title: Maxwell's Demon
nodes.description: A thought-experiment demon that sorts fast and slow molecules — connecting thermodynamics and information.

nodes.title: Gravity Well
nodes.description: Newtonian gravity as a curved potential well — orbits, escape velocity, and tidal forces.

nodes.title: Three-Body Problem
nodes.description: Three mutually attracting masses — Poincaré proved no closed-form solution exists, a landmark in chaos theory.

nodes.title: Harmonic Oscillator Phase Space
nodes.description: The simple harmonic oscillator traced in phase space — elliptical orbits, energy surfaces, and damping spirals.

nodes.title: Double Pendulum Chaos
nodes.description: An array of double pendula launched from nearly identical conditions — sensitive dependence made visible.

nodes.title: Lorenz Attractor
nodes.description: Lorenz's strange attractor — deterministic chaos in a three-variable weather model.

nodes.title: Kicked Pendulum
nodes.description: The Chirikov standard map — periodic kicks drive a pendulum from regular orbits to global chaos.

nodes.title: Dripping Faucet
nodes.description: The logistic map governs drip timing — period-doubling route to chaos in a kitchen sink.

nodes.title: Energy Landscape
nodes.description: Minima, saddle points, and basins of attraction — the geometry of stability and transitions.

nodes.title: Quantization
nodes.description: Energy, charge, and angular momentum come in discrete indivisible packets. Planck's 1900 fix for the ultraviolet catastrophe revealed that nature is fundamentally granular.

nodes.title: Wave-Particle Duality
nodes.description: Quantum objects exhibit wave or particle character depending on what is measured — never both simultaneously. de Broglie (1924) showed every particle carries a wavelength λ = h/p.

nodes.title: de Broglie Relation
nodes.description: Every particle has an associated wavelength λ = h/p. This wave-like property governs interference, diffraction, and quantized energy levels in confined systems.

nodes.title: Superposition
nodes.description: A quantum system exists in a linear combination of eigenstates until measured. Schrödinger's equation is linear, so any sum of solutions is also a valid solution.

nodes.title: Complementarity
nodes.description: Bohr's principle: wave and particle descriptions are mutually exclusive — gaining which-path information destroys the interference pattern.

nodes.title: Measurement & Collapse
nodes.description: The act of measurement selects a definite outcome from a superposition, collapsing the wavefunction. It is the measurement interaction itself — not physical disturbance — that destroys coherence.

nodes.title: Tunneling & Barriers
nodes.description: Quantum wavefunctions have non-zero amplitude inside classically forbidden regions. Gamow (1928) used this to explain alpha decay; it underlies tunnel diodes, STM, and nuclear fusion in stars.

nodes.title: Entanglement
nodes.description: Einstein's 'spooky action at a distance': pairs of particles share a joint quantum state that cannot be factored into independent parts, regardless of the distance between them.

nodes.title: Phase Space
nodes.description: The state of a mechanical system is a single point in a space spanned by positions and momenta. Liouville's theorem says phase-space volume is conserved; chaos stretches and folds it.

nodes.title: Chaos & Sensitivity
nodes.description: Deterministic systems can be unpredictable: infinitesimally close initial conditions diverge exponentially (positive Lyapunov exponent). Long-term forecasting becomes impossible without infinite precision.

nodes.title: Entropy (S = k ln W)
nodes.description: Boltzmann's statistical definition: entropy counts the number of microscopic arrangements W compatible with a macroscopic state. The most probable state has the most microstates.

nodes.title: Arrow of Time
nodes.description: The second law gives time a direction: entropy of an isolated system almost always increases. Microscopic laws are time-reversible; the arrow is statistical, not fundamental.

nodes.title: Kinetic Theory
nodes.description: Macroscopic properties — pressure, temperature, viscosity — emerge from the statistical mechanics of molecular collisions. Maxwell and Boltzmann derived the equilibrium speed distribution from first principles.

nodes.title: Ergodicity
nodes.description: Over infinite time an ergodic system visits all accessible microstates with equal frequency, so time averages equal ensemble averages. Most real systems are only ergodic under restricted conditions.

nodes.title: Landauer Erasure
nodes.description: Rolf Landauer proved that erasing one bit of information must dissipate at least kT ln 2 of heat. Information is not abstract — it is physical, and its destruction has a thermodynamic cost.

nodes.title: Shannon Information
nodes.description: Shannon's entropy H = −Σ p log p quantifies the minimum bits needed to encode a message. The mathematical structure mirrors Boltzmann's thermodynamic entropy — a deep, still-debated connection.

nodes.title: Information is Physical
nodes.description: Rolf Landauer's maxim: 'Information is physical.' Acquiring, storing, and erasing information all have thermodynamic consequences. This unification underlies quantum computing and quantum cryptography.
